title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the
indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input has **exactly one solution**, and you may not
use the same element twice. You can return the answer in any order.

solutions.summary: Check every pair of indices until a matching sum is found.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `[]` return, so the function is valid before we add any logic.

steps.title: Loop over the first number
steps.explanation: Walk an index `i` across the array. This is the first of the two numbers in each pair.

steps.title: Scan every later number
steps.explanation: For each `i`, loop a second index `j` over the elements after it, so every pair is checked exactly once.

steps.title: Return the matching pair
steps.explanation: If `nums[i] + nums[j]` equals the target, those indices are the answer.

solutions.summary: Remember each value as you go; look up the complement in O(1).

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Same starting point: signature plus a default `[]` return.

steps.title: Create a lookup map
steps.explanation: Use a hash map from a value to the index where we saw it. Lookups in it are O(1).

steps.title: Walk the array once
steps.explanation: Visit each value `n` together with its index `i`.

steps.title: Compute the complement
steps.explanation: The partner we need for the current number is `target - n` — the value that would complete the sum.

steps.title: Return when the complement was already seen
steps.explanation: If the complement is in the map, we have found both numbers — return its stored index and the current one.

steps.title: Remember the current value
steps.explanation: If there was no match, record the current value and its index so a later number can find it.
